query: We are launching a new smart mirror that currently just shows the time and weather. I want to build a platform strategy that allows developers to create their own 'mirror-apps' and turn it into a hub for home health and fashion. How should I architect this to maximize innovation?

I'm building a cloud-based IDE for junior developers. Instead of just fixing their bugs, I want to create an ecosystem where they can build and share their own 'automation recipes'. How can I apply generativity to make this a thriving community?

Our enterprise search tool is too rigid. I want to transform it into a system where different departments can build their own 'knowledge extractors' and 'data connectors' without us having to build every integration ourselves. What are the key architectural layers I need?

I'm designing a marketplace for custom board games. I want to make sure the game designers feel like entrepreneurs and can build long-term businesses on our platform. What should I consider for value capture and residual claimancy?

We have a fleet of autonomous delivery robots. Right now they just deliver food. I want to open up their 'sensibility' and 'programmability' to third-party developers so they can use the robots for things like street maintenance reports or mobile security. How do I balance control and generativity?
I'm building a new social network based on 'shared mini-games'. I want the users to be able to create their own games and monetize them. How do I start the 'generativity flywheel' for this platform?

Our CRM is too basic. I want to reframe it from a 'single-purpose fix' to a platform that enables our customers to innovate. What does 'meta-problem solving' look like in the context of sales and relationship management?

I'm architecting a new API for a fitness tracking wearable. I want to ensure it's high-leverage and adaptable. How can I use the 7 digital properties to make it a foundation for unpredicted innovation?

We want to build a 'Lego for Finance' platform where fintech startups can combine different modules to create new financial products. What should our 'Allocation Layer' and 'Generation Layer' look like?

I have this idea for a 'Laundry Buddy' moment for our smart fridge. Instead of just tracking food, I want it to be a platform for 'kitchen innovations' we haven't predicted yet. How do I design for 'surprise as a feature'?
Can you help me write a Python script to scrape news headlines from a list of 50 websites and save them to a CSV file?
I'm having trouble with my React component. The state isn't updating correctly when I click the button. Here's the code...
Can you summarize the main arguments of the book 'The Lean Startup' by Eric Ries and compare them to traditional business planning?
I need a formal email template to send to my boss requesting a promotion and a salary increase.
What are the best practices for setting up a CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions for a Node.js project?
How do I calculate the Weighted Average Cost of Capital (WACC) for a publicly traded company? Please provide the formula and a step-by-step example.
I'm trying to optimize my SQL query. It's taking 10 seconds to join these three large tables. Can you suggest some indexing strategies?
Can you explain the difference between a 'monolith' and a 'microservices' architecture in terms of deployment and scalability?
I want to create a simple to-do list app in Flutter. Can you give me the basic project structure and a few screens?
What are the legal requirements for GDPR compliance when collecting user email addresses for a newsletter?
